design.summary: M14 = fill the already-minted, identity-only `ObjKind::Channel` endpoint (caps.rs) with a real body: a kernel-owned, bounded, ordered, bidirectional message stream over which agents MOVE capabilities. Structural model = Zircon channel (async, two single-owner endpoints, per-direction FIFO, handles moved not copied, peer-closed); attenuation/identity rules = seL4 (received rights are a subset, gated on the presented cap's TRANSFER right, no ambient PID); mechanism = 100% the M11 primitives already green. The capability MOVE is literally `HandleTable::transfer_to` (caps.rs:532) DECOMPOSED ACROSS TIME via a shared `Rc<ipc::Channel>`: the send half does `live+clone+free_slot` (the cap goes STALE in the sender), the recv half does `alloc` into the receiver — object identity (the `Rc<Object>`) and rights ride intact, attenuated first via the existing `narrow`/`M_HANDLE_NARROW`. Everything routes through the single `caps::dispatch` chokepoint (caps.rs:548) gated by the existing `required_right` table; bound enforced fail-closed (`SysStatus::WouldBlock = 5`, already reserved "for M14 channels" at caps.rs:184). ZERO new unsafe: a new `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` module `ipc.rs` (VecDeque+RefCell+Rc, the exact M13 mem.rs pattern), two safe half-primitives on `HandleTable`, two dispatch arms, three kernel-side lib.rs facades, and a kernel self-test that asserts FIFO + cap-moved-and-attenuated + full->WouldBlock + denied paths, then prints "M14: ipc OK". The byte-buffer (copy_to_user) generalization is the ONLY thing that would add unsafe and is explicitly DEFERRED — the M14 marker passes on an inline-scalar payload with zero new unsafe.
design.abi:
M_CHAN_* ABI on the pointer-free [u64;4] SyscallArgs through the M11 dispatch chokepoint.

Method numbers (caps.rs; highest used = M_MEM_READ=24, next free 25/26/27):
  `pub const M_CHAN_SEND: u32 = 25;`  -> required_right => Rights::WRITE
  `pub const M_CHAN_RECV: u32 = 26;`  -> required_right => Rights::READ
  `pub const M_CHAN_CLOSE: u32 = 27;` -> required_right => Rights::NONE (closing your own endpoint always allowed)
Add all three arms to the `required_right(method)` match (caps.rs:335) so the closed set stays closed (unknown 28 -> BadMethod).

Encoding on `SyscallArgs{ method:u32, handle:Handle, args:[u64;4] }`:
  M_CHAN_SEND: `handle` = endpoint cap (resolved in caller's table, gate WRITE). `args[0]` = inline scalar payload. `args[1]` = raw handle of the cap to transfer (0 / Handle::NULL = bytes-only). args[2..3] reserved for the future copy_to_user user-buf ptr/len. Returns SysReturn::status(Ok) | WouldBlock (outbox full) | PeerClosed | Denied (no WRITE on endpoint, or carried cap lacks TRANSFER) | BadCap (handle not an ObjKind::Channel endpoint, i.e. `chan == None`).
  M_CHAN_RECV: `handle` = endpoint (gate READ). args reserved (flags / user buf). Returns SysReturn::ok(moved_handle_raw) — `value` = the raw handle freshly `attach`-ed into the caller's table for the moved capability (0 if the message carried none); WouldBlock if inbox empty & peer open; PeerClosed if inbox empty & peer closed.
  M_CHAN_CLOSE: `handle` = endpoint; flips `Channel::close_side(side)`; Ok.

SINGLE-SCALAR SysReturn carries ONE u64. recv returns the MOVED HANDLE in `value`; the inline payload is delivered (a) at the real user ABI by copy_to_user into an args-pointed buffer (DEFERRED, the only future unsafe) and (b) kernel-side for the self-test by a richer facade tuple. No change to `agent_syscall_current` (lib.rs:1363) is needed for the inline increment — it already plumbs (method,handle,a0=payload,a1=cap-handle).

Dispatch arms (inside caps::dispatch, after the WRITE/READ gate already applied; mirror the M_MEM_* "clone the Rc<Object> out, DROP the slot borrow, then borrow_mut the Ring" discipline so `&mut table` never aliases the in-flight RefCell borrow — single-core, interrupts masked, no reentrant borrow):
  M_CHAN_SEND: clone endpoint `Rc<Object>` out of `slots[i]`; read `(chan, side)` from its `chan` field (None -> BadCap). If `!chan.peer_open(side)` -> PeerClosed. `let full = chan.outbox(side).borrow().q.len() >= chan.outbox(side).borrow().cap;` if full -> WouldBlock (NOTHING detached yet). If `args[1]!=0`: resolve it, require Rights::TRANSFER (else Denied), reject self-channel, then `table.detach(cap_h)` -> parked. `chan.outbox(side).borrow_mut().q.push_back(Message{payload:args[0], cap:parked});` Ok.
  M_CHAN_RECV: clone endpoint Rc out; read `(chan,side)`. `let msg = chan.inbox(side).borrow_mut().q.pop_front();` (drop the ring borrow) -> None: if `!chan.peer_open(side)` PeerClosed else WouldBlock. Some(msg): if `msg.cap` -> `table.attach(parked)` -> fresh Handle; return ok(handle.raw()) (payload surfaced via the facade tuple), else ok(0).
design.ipc_module:
WHERE THE SAFE MODULE LIVES + the exact integration points.

NEW FILE: C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\ipc.rs — `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` header (mirror mem.rs:1). Contains Message/Ring/Channel + `create()` + the accessor methods above. Uses only `alloc::collections::VecDeque`, `alloc::rc::Rc`, `core::cell::{RefCell,Cell}` — all already in use by the green M13 substrate; ZERO new dependency, ZERO new unsafe.

crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs
  - :42 add `mod ipc;` after `mod mem;`.
  - :1391 region — add three kernel-side facades next to agent_mem_dispatch (the AGENTS registry is private; the facades are the only door). Sequence the two table borrows so AGENTS.slot() never hands out two overlapping `&mut` (see risks):
      `pub fn agent_channel_connect(a: Task, b: Task, bound: usize) -> Option<(caps::Handle, caps::Handle)>` — `let ch = ipc::create(bound);` then in two SEPARATE borrow scopes: borrow A's slot, `ha = ap_a.table.mint_channel_endpoint(READ|WRITE|TRANSFER, ch.clone(), 0)`; drop; borrow B's slot, `hb = ap_b.table.mint_channel_endpoint(READ|WRITE|TRANSFER, ch, 1)`; return (ha,hb).
      `pub fn agent_chan_send(t: Task, ep: caps::Handle, payload: u64, cap_h: u64) -> Option<(u32,u64)>` — dispatch M_CHAN_SEND with args [payload, cap_h, 0, 0] against the task's OWN table; returns (status,value).
      `pub fn agent_chan_recv(t: Task, ep: caps::Handle) -> Option<(u32,u64)>` — dispatch M_CHAN_RECV; returns (status, moved_handle). (Inline payload read kernel-side: for the self-test, peek `chan.inbox(side)` front payload before/around recv, OR add a thin `agent_chan_recv_full(t,ep)->Option<(u32,u64,u64)>` reading payload+handle inside one borrow — recommended, mirrors agent_mem_dispatch returning value.)
  - :1270 agent_spawn — OPTIONAL: today bootstrap = `table.mint(ObjKind::Channel, bs_rights)` (identity stub, chan:None). M14 can leave it (agent_born_ok:1417 still inspects it as Channel) and mint real endpoints via agent_channel_connect, OR rewrite spawn so two siblings' bootstraps share a Channel. RECOMMEND: leave the stub, connect explicitly — non-invasive, keeps M12 green.

crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs (stays `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`)
  - Object (:218) — add private `chan: Option<(Rc<crate::ipc::Channel>, u8)>`; update mint:441, mint_memory_home:457, mint_record:471 with `chan: None`; add `mint_channel_endpoint`.
  - method consts — add M_CHAN_SEND=25/RECV=26/CLOSE=27; extend required_right:335.
  - SysStatus — add `PeerClosed = 8` after `ObjFull = 7`.
  - HandleTable — add pub(crate) `detach`/`attach`; refactor `transfer_to`:532 to call them.
  - dispatch:548 — add the three M_CHAN_* arms.

kernel/src/main.rs — self-test block replaces the post-M13 `tb_hal::halt()` at :1534; new marker "M14: ipc OK\n".
design.selftest:
KERNEL SELF-TEST (multi-agent north star), mirroring the M13 `disp`/`m13_fail` block (main.rs:1376-1532). REUSE agent_c (slot from agent_spawn @ main.rs:1495) and agent_d (:1496) as the two IPC peers — both are already live agents born with a Channel bootstrap, so NO new task slots and NO MAX_TASKS bump (MAX_TASKS=12 is nearly full: boot=0, scheduler_spawn C/D, then agents a/b/c/d). Slot in BEFORE the final `tb_hal::halt()` (main.rs:1534); reuse an `m14_fail(why)->!` fatal (same shape as m13_fail:1376) so a broken invariant parks with NO marker (runner grep fails loudly).

Steps + concrete asserts (let ok = SysStatus::Ok as u32; wb = SysStatus::WouldBlock as u32; den = SysStatus::Denied as u32):
1. CONNECT: `let (ep_a, ep_b) = tb_hal::agent_channel_connect(agent_c, agent_d, 2).unwrap_or_else(|| m14_fail("connect"));` — one Channel, ep_a (side 0) in C's table, ep_b (side 1) in D's, Rc-shared, bound=2.
2. MINT + NARROW a carried cap in C's table holding READ|TRANSFER (use agent_mem_dispatch path or a direct mint of an ObjKind::Generic with READ|TRANSFER), then NARROW it to READ-only via M_HANDLE_NARROW — the derived-narrowed capability. Call its raw value `cap_h`.
3. SEND #1 (cap-carrying): `let (s,_) = agent_chan_send(agent_c, ep_a, 0xCAFE, cap_h).unwrap(); assert s==ok;` Then assert the cap is now STALE in C: M_OBJECT_INSPECT on cap_h in C's table -> status == Stale ("A no longer can").
4. SEND #2 (bytes-only): `agent_chan_send(agent_c, ep_a, 0xF00D, 0)` -> ok.
5. RECV #1 in D: `let (s, mh) = agent_chan_recv_full(agent_d, ep_b) -> (s, payload, moved_h);` assert s==ok; assert payload==0xCAFE; assert moved_h != 0; assert M_OBJECT_INSPECT(moved_h) in D's table == Ok with the right ObjKind; assert rights of moved_h contain READ but NOT TRANSFER (attenuation held cross-agent); B USES it: a READ-gated method on moved_h -> Ok.
6. FIFO: RECV #2 in D -> (ok, 0xF00D, no-cap). Proves 0xCAFE delivered BEFORE 0xF00D (per-direction FIFO).
7. EMPTY: RECV #3 on empty inbox -> status == wb (WouldBlock), peer still open.
8. BACKPRESSURE: send into C's outbox until full (bound=2): two ok sends, the 3rd send -> status == wb; assert NO cap stranded (do the full check with a TRANSFER-carrying send and confirm the carried cap is STILL live in C's table after the WouldBlock — the atomic check-before-detach).
9. DENIED / non-channel paths: (a) M_CHAN_SEND on agent_c's memory_home handle -> passes the WRITE gate but the body sees chan==None -> BadCap; (b) NARROW ep_a to drop WRITE, M_CHAN_SEND on it -> den (Denied at the required_right gate); (c) carried cap lacking TRANSFER -> den; (d) unknown method 28 -> BadMethod.
10. Print witnesses: "ipc: FIFO + capability moved + attenuated cross-agent\n" and "ipc: full channel WouldBlock + non-channel BadCap + denied paths\n", then `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M14: ipc OK\n");` then `tb_hal::halt()`.

The disarmed-timer test observes the CLOSED-STATUS form (WouldBlock/PeerClosed) directly; the recv-blocks-off-the-runqueue / send-wakes-peer scheduler round-trip is the additive Step-2 layer the same milestone wires but a disarmed-timer self-test cannot exercise (state this; do not claim it).
design.new_unsafe:
ZERO new unsafe — CONFIRMED for the M14 inline-scalar increment that earns the marker.

- ipc.rs: `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at the top — VecDeque + RefCell + Cell + Rc are all safe heap; the file cannot compile any unsafe. This is the identical soundness argument the green M13 mem.rs already discharges (Object holds `Option<RefCell<MemSubstrate>>` reached through a shared `Rc<Object>`; sound because dispatch runs single-core with interrupts masked -> no reentrant borrow). A `RefCell<VecDeque<Message>>` reuses that exact already-blessed pattern — no atomics, no lock, no `&'static mut` (so no heapless/ringbuf/crossbeam dependency, which would add atomics that buy nothing on single-core).
- caps.rs: already `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (line 1). The additions (the `chan` Object field, `detach`/`attach`, the `transfer_to` refactor, the M_CHAN_* dispatch arms, `SysStatus::PeerClosed`, `mint_channel_endpoint`) are all pure safe Rust under that forbid — the compiler enforces it.
- lib.rs facades (agent_channel_connect / agent_chan_send / agent_chan_recv): use the EXISTING `AGENTS.slot()` accessor (whose single `unsafe impl Sync` + UnsafeCell already exist for M12/M13 and add NO new unsafe block); the facades themselves are safe calls into caps::dispatch, exactly like agent_mem_dispatch.
- main.rs self-test: no unsafe.

The ONLY thing in the ROADMAP M14 scope that would introduce unsafe is the variable-length BYTE payload via copy_to_user/copy_from_user across the two address spaces (bounds+mapping-checked cross-space copy in arch::*). That is DELIBERATELY DEFERRED: the inline-scalar message (payload in args[0], cap moved by handle) carries the marker self-test with zero new unsafe. Be explicit in the milestone notes that "M14: ipc OK" is claimed on the zero-new-unsafe inline path, and the copy_to_user byte enlargement lands later as an isolated, audited unsafe increment — do NOT claim the full ROADMAP M14 byte-buffer surface at zero unsafe.
risks: ATOMICITY of the cap move on a full ring: if you detach the carried cap from the sender's table THEN find the outbox Ring full, the cap is lost (already free_slot'd). MUST check Ring space (would-not-block) BEFORE detach, or re-attach on WouldBlock. transfer_to is all-or-nothing; the split send/recv MUST preserve that. Self-test step 8 asserts the carried cap is still live in C after a WouldBlock send.

BORROW aliasing (RefCell panics at runtime, not compile-fail): dispatch holds `&mut table`; reaching the endpoint's `Rc<Channel>` and borrow_mut-ing its Ring while also detach/attach-ing the SAME table can double-borrow. Clone the endpoint `Rc<Object>` OUT and drop the slots[i] borrow BEFORE touching the Ring (the exact M_MEM_* discipline at caps.rs dispatch), and finish the Ring borrow (pop the Message out) before table.attach.

TWO overlapping &mut from AGENTS.slot(): agent_channel_connect needs to mint into BOTH agent A's and agent B's tables. AGENTS.slot(i) hands out a transient `&mut Option<AgentProcess>` from one UnsafeCell-backed array; calling it for two indices and holding both live violates the single-&mut-at-a-time discipline `unsafe impl Sync for AgentTable` relies on. Create the `Rc<Channel>` first, then mint endpoint0 in a borrow scope that ENDS before borrowing B's slot for endpoint1 (clone the Rc across).

Endpoint side bookkeeping: dir[side]=outbox, dir[1-side]=inbox. Getting the flip wrong makes an agent read its own sends (echo) or deadlock. connect MUST mint side=0 and side=1 against the SAME Rc<Channel>; the self-test (C=side0 sends, D=side1 recvs) catches an echo bug because RECV would return WouldBlock instead of 0xCAFE.

Object field ripple: the Object literal is built in 3 caps.rs constructors (mint:441, mint_memory_home:457, the PRIVATE mint_record:471). Adding `chan` requires `chan: None` in all three or the crate won't compile — easy to miss mint_record.

Single-scalar SysReturn vs two outputs: recv must return BOTH the inline payload AND the moved handle, but SysReturn{status,value} holds one u64. Resolution: value=moved handle; payload via copy_to_user (deferred) or a kernel-side facade tuple (status,payload,handle). Do NOT try to pack both into one scalar.

ZERO-new-unsafe scope boundary: the inline-scalar increment is genuinely zero new unsafe, but ROADMAP-V2.md scopes M14 to also include real BYTE payloads via copy_to_user/copy_from_user (the ONLY M14 unsafe). The marker-passing self-test deliberately defers that; don't claim full ROADMAP M14 byte buffers at zero unsafe.

Scheduler block/wake not testable with the timer disarmed: ROADMAP wants recv-on-empty to park off the M9 RUNQUEUE and send to make the peer runnable. The disarmed-timer self-test can only observe the CLOSED-STATUS WouldBlock/PeerClosed form, not the off-runqueue block+wake round-trip. State this so M14 isn't claimed to prove the IPC<->scheduler wake path it cannot exercise here; design Step-1 (WouldBlock) so Step-2 (the `waiter` slot + wake-at-send) is purely additive and lost-wakeup-safe inside the same interrupts-masked critical section.

Adding SysStatus::PeerClosed=8 extends the closed status enum: it is safe Rust (no unsafe) but any exhaustive match on SysStatus elsewhere must handle it, and the runner/markers must not regress — keep it the last variant after ObjFull=7.

recon.channel_object:
CHANNEL OBJECT SHAPE + WHERE IT LIVES.

The `ObjKind::Channel` variant ALREADY exists (caps.rs:213, "An IPC channel endpoint (M14)") and is the kind each agent's bootstrap handle is minted with today (lib.rs:1293 `table.mint(caps::ObjKind::Channel, bs_rights)`), but it is currently an IDENTITY-ONLY STUB: `Object { kind: Channel, mem: None, scalar: 0 }` with no payload. `SysStatus::WouldBlock = 5` is ALSO pre-reserved ("reserved for M14 channels", caps.rs SysStatus). So M14 is a body-fill, not an ABI break — exactly as ROADMAP-V2.md:381 states.

RECOMMENDED placement: a NEW safe module `crates/tb-hal/src/ipc.rs` headed `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, declared `mod ipc;` in lib.rs right after `mod mem;` (lib.rs:42) — the EXACT precedent M13 set (mem.rs is `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, referenced from caps.rs `Object.mem: Option<RefCell<crate::mem::MemSubstrate>>`). The channel core stays in ipc.rs; caps.rs only gains the endpoint payload field + two cross-table half-primitives (see transfer_path).

Endpoint/peers + ordered bounded queue (Zircon two-endpoints-share-one-core model):
```rust
// ipc.rs  #![forbid(unsafe_code)]
use alloc::collections::VecDeque; use alloc::rc::Rc; use core::cell::RefCell;

pub struct Message {                 // one in-transit message
    pub payload: u64,                // inline scalar (the "bytes" at M14 inline-ABI)
    pub cap: Option<(Rc<crate::caps::Object>, crate::caps::Rights)>, // a PARKED capability
}
struct Ring { q: VecDeque<Message>, cap: usize } // ordered + BOUNDED (cap = backpressure)
pub struct Channel { dir: [RefCell<Ring>; 2] }   // dir[0]=side0->side1, dir[1]=side1->side0
pub fn create(bound: usize) -> Rc<Channel> { /* two empty bounded rings */ }
```
The Object payload is the endpoint: `chan: Option<(Rc<ipc::Channel>, u8 /*side*/)>` added to caps.rs `Object` (caps.rs:220), parallel to `mem`. Endpoint(side).outbox = `dir[side]`, inbox = `dir[1-side]`. BOTH endpoint Objects — one in agent A's HandleTable, one in agent B's — hold an `Rc` clone of the SAME `Channel`, so the two per-principal tables stay disjoint (no shared global namespace) yet rendezvous on the Rc. Object is `pub struct` (caps.rs:220) with private fields, so ipc.rs holding `Rc<caps::Object>` is opaque/legal. The 3 Object constructors (mint caps.rs:441, mint_memory_home:457, mint_record) each gain `chan: None` — mechanical, caps.rs-local. ZERO new unsafe: VecDeque + RefCell + Rc are all safe heap (mem.rs already uses alloc::collections + RefCell under forbid(unsafe_code)).
recon.transfer_path:
HOW A MESSAGE MOVES A CAPABILITY (reuse M11 transfer, split across the ring).

M11 already has the whole-move primitive `HandleTable::transfer_to(&mut self, h, dst: &mut HandleTable) -> Option<Handle>` (caps.rs:532): `live(h)` -> clone `rights` + `object: Rc<Object>` -> `dst.alloc(rights, object)` -> `free_slot(i)` on source. The object is never lost (Rc keeps it alive; raw handle value never reused across tables; receiver gets a fresh slot+generation). The CHANNEL move is that SAME transfer DECOMPOSED ACROSS TIME via the shared Rc<Channel> ring — crucially, send and recv each touch ONLY the CURRENT agent's own table, never the peer's:

  SEND (in agent A's table): the FIRST half of transfer_to — `detach(cap_h) -> (Rc<Object>, Rights)` = `live` + clone object/rights + `free_slot` (cap is now STALE in A; A "no longer can"). The (Rc<Object>, Rights) is PARKED inside `Message.cap` and pushed into the endpoint's outbox Ring. While parked, NO table holds a handle to it — the Rc alone keeps it alive.
  RECV (in agent B's table): the SECOND half — pop the Message, `attach((Rc<Object>, Rights)) -> Handle` = `alloc(rights, object)` into B's table, yielding a fresh slot+generation handle. Object identity (the Rc) is preserved end-to-end; rights ride along intact, or are DUP-ATTENUATED first (sender narrows before send — the "derived-narrowed capability" of ROADMAP M14).

Add to caps.rs (pub(crate), used by the lib.rs facade / by dispatch):
  `pub(crate) fn detach(&mut self, h) -> Result<(Rc<Object>, Rights), SysStatus>` (live -> clone -> free_slot)
  `pub(crate) fn attach(&mut self, parked: (Rc<Object>, Rights)) -> Option<Handle>` (alloc)
and refactor `transfer_to` to `self.detach(h)` + `dst.attach(parked)` — proving the channel move IS the M11 transfer. Rights gate: the carried cap must hold `Rights::TRANSFER` (same gate as M_HANDLE_TRANSFER, caps.rs required_right). ATOMICITY: check the outbox Ring has space (would-not-block) BEFORE `detach`, so a WouldBlock send never strands a detached cap. Zero-ambient-authority preserved: dispatch acts only on the presented endpoint's rights + the presented cap's TRANSFER right.
recon.abi_methods:
M_CHAN_* ABI ON THE POINTER-FREE [u64;4] SyscallArgs.

Method-number space (caps.rs): meta-ops [0,16), agent verbs [16,..). Highest used = M_MEM_READ=24. Next free = 25,26:
  `pub const M_CHAN_SEND: u32 = 25;`  required_right -> `Rights::WRITE` (write into the channel)
  `pub const M_CHAN_RECV: u32 = 26;`  required_right -> `Rights::READ`  (read from the channel)
Both added to the `required_right(method)` match (caps.rs) so the closed method set stays closed (unknown 27 -> BadMethod). Both are implementable INSIDE `caps::dispatch` (caps.rs dispatch match) exactly like M_MEM_* — because send/recv touch only the CURRENT table + the Rc-shared Channel, no peer table needed.

SyscallArgs encoding (`method:u32, handle:Handle, args:[u64;4]`):
  M_CHAN_SEND: `handle` = the endpoint capability (resolved in caller's table, gate WRITE). `args[0]` = inline scalar payload. `args[1]` = raw handle of the capability to transfer (0 / Handle::NULL = no cap). args[2..3] reserved (a future user buffer ptr/len for the copy_to_user byte path). Returns SysReturn::status(Ok) or WouldBlock (full) / Denied (no WRITE) / BadCap (handle is not a Channel) / Denied (carried cap lacks TRANSFER).
  M_CHAN_RECV: `handle` = the endpoint (gate READ). args[0..] reserved (flags / user buffer). Returns SysReturn::ok(moved_handle_raw) where value = the raw handle freshly installed into the caller's table for the moved capability (0 if the message carried none); WouldBlock if the inbox ring is empty.

PAYLOAD-vs-HANDLE on a single-scalar SysReturn: `SysReturn{status,value}` carries one u64, so recv returns the MOVED HANDLE in `value`; the inline scalar payload is delivered (a) at the user ABI via copy_to_user into args-pointed buffer (the ONLY new unsafe, deferred — see risks), and (b) kernel-side for the self-test via a richer facade tuple `agent_chan_recv(task,ep) -> Option<(status,payload,handle)>` mirroring `agent_mem_dispatch` (lib.rs:1391). The existing user shim `agent_syscall_current(method,handle,a0,a1)` (lib.rs:1363) already plumbs the two send args (a0=payload, a1=cap-handle) — no shim signature change needed for the inline-scalar increment.

New lib.rs facades (kernel-side, mirroring agent_mem_dispatch's AGENTS-private access):
  `agent_channel_connect(a: Task, b: Task, bound) -> Option<(Handle,Handle)>` — ipc::create(bound), mint endpoint0 into A's table + endpoint1 into B's table (or FILL the born-with bootstrap bodies: rewrite agent_spawn so A.bootstrap and B.bootstrap share a Channel).
  `agent_chan_send(t,ep,payload,cap_h) -> Option<(u32,u64)>` and `agent_chan_recv(t,ep) -> Option<(u32,u64,u64)>` — run caps::dispatch against the task's OWN table.
recon.message_encoding:
MESSAGE ENCODING: inline scalar payload + optional moved handle.

At the M14 inline-ABI increment (the recon's scope, ZERO new unsafe), a message = `Message{ payload: u64, cap: Option<(Rc<caps::Object>, caps::Rights)> }`. payload is the inline scalar carried in `args[0]` on send and returned (kernel-side) by the recv facade tuple; cap is the parked, in-transit capability (None for a bytes-only message). This matches the prompt's "inline scalar payload + an optional handle to transfer" and reuses the pointer-free [u64;4] SyscallArgs unchanged — buffers were explicitly deferred to M14 (SyscallArgs doc-comment caps.rs: "Pointer-free at M11 (buffers arrive with M14 channels)").

The carried capability is encoded by HANDLE on the wire-in (sender's `args[1]` = raw handle in sender's table) and by FRESH HANDLE on the wire-out (recv's `value` = raw handle in receiver's table). Handles are process-local (caps.rs Handle doc: "meaningless in any other principal's table, Zircon model"), so the integer is NEVER copied across tables — only the Object identity (Rc) + Rights cross, re-slotted/re-generationed on attach. A NULL cap (args[1]==0, Handle(0) reserved invalid) means bytes-only.

The REAL byte payload (variable-length, not just a scalar) is the M14 generalization noted in ROADMAP-V2.md:240 — delivered via copy_to_user/copy_from_user across the two address spaces (args[2]=user buf ptr, args[3]=len). That path is the ONLY thing in M14 that introduces unsafe (bounds+mapping-checked cross-space copy in arch::*), and is NOT exercised by the inline-scalar self-test. ROADMAP-V2.md:381 notes M14 "lights up the M13 copy_to_user/copy_from_user buffer payload path the pointer-free M13 ABI was designed to forward to."
recon.ordering_backpressure:
ORDERING (FIFO) + BACKPRESSURE (bounded -> WouldBlock).

ORDERING: each direction is its own FIFO `VecDeque<Message>` — `send` = push_back, `recv` = pop_front -> strict per-channel-per-direction FIFO. Two independent rings (`dir[0]`, `dir[1]`) means A->B and B->A never interleave, and "one stream breaking does not affect another" (AGENTS-SPEC §6 A2A rule). No reordering is possible: there is no priority, no key, no reinsertion.

BACKPRESSURE: `Ring{ q, cap }` bounds depth at `cap`. `send` into a full ring returns `SysStatus::WouldBlock` (caps.rs:SysStatus, value 5, the pre-reserved "reserved for M14 channels" status) — NEVER unbounded buffering, fail-closed. `recv` on an empty ring also returns `WouldBlock`. This is the kernel-side closed-status surface; the SCHEDULER integration (ROADMAP-V2.md:232 "recv on an empty channel blocks the agent off the M9 run queue; send makes it runnable — the IPC<->scheduler wake path") is the runnable-wake generalization layered on top: recv-on-empty parks the task off RUNQUEUE, send flips the peer back onto it. The self-test (timer disarmed, single-core) observes the CLOSED-STATUS form directly (WouldBlock), not the block/wake; the block/wake path is the M9 run-queue integration the same milestone wires but which a disarmed-timer kernel self-test cannot round-trip. Borrow discipline (single-core, interrupts masked) matches the M13 RefCell argument: clone the endpoint's `Rc<Channel>` out of the slot and drop the slot borrow BEFORE `borrow_mut()`-ing the Ring (caps.rs dispatch M_MEM_* already does exactly this "clone the Rc out, drop the borrow" dance), so no reentrant borrow_mut and no aliasing &mut on the table.
recon.selftest_plan:
KERNEL SELF-TEST (the multi-agent north star), mirroring M13 Witness-B + the `disp` helper.

Slot in main.rs: REPLACE the `tb_hal::halt()` at main.rs:1534 (immediately after `"M13: memory OK\n"`, main.rs:1532) with an M14 `{ ... }` block + `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M14: ipc OK\n");` + `tb_hal::halt()`. Reuse the m13-style `m14_fail(why) -> !` fatal + report pattern (main.rs:1376) so a broken invariant parks the core with NO marker (runner grep fails loudly).

SLOT BUDGET (MAX_TASKS=12, lib.rs:216): boot=0; M2 A/B=1,2; M9 C/D=3,4; M10 G/H=5,6; M12 a/b=7,8; M13 c/d=9,10 -> only slot 11 free. So REUSE the already-spawned M13 agents agent_c (slot 9) + agent_d (slot 10) as the two IPC peers (each already born with an ObjKind::Channel bootstrap, agent_born_ok already asserts it) — OR bump MAX_TASKS to 14 and add STACK_AGENT_E/F (like main.rs:182-184) before spawning two fresh agents. Reuse is cleaner and avoids slot pressure.

Steps:
1. CONNECT: `let (ep_a, ep_b) = agent_channel_connect(agent_c, agent_d, /*bound*/2)` — one Channel, endpoint0 in C's table, endpoint1 in D's table (Rc-shared).
2. MINT + NARROW a capability in C's table with READ|TRANSFER (e.g. a MemoryRecord/Budget/Generic), then dup-attenuate via M_HANDLE_NARROW to READ-only — the "derived-narrowed capability".
3. SEND #1: `agent_chan_send(agent_c, ep_a, payload=0xCAFE, cap=narrowed_h)` -> Ok. Assert the cap handle is now STALE in C's table (M_OBJECT_INSPECT on it -> Stale: "A no longer can").
4. ORDERING setup: SEND #2 `agent_chan_send(agent_c, ep_a, 0xF00D, NULL)` -> Ok (no cap).
5. RECV #1: `agent_chan_recv(agent_d, ep_b)` -> (Ok, payload=0xCAFE, moved_h). Assert payload==0xCAFE; moved_h resolves in D's table; M_OBJECT_INSPECT(moved_h)->Ok with the right ObjKind; rights = READ but NOT TRANSFER (attenuation held); B USES it (a READ-gated method -> Ok).
6. FIFO: RECV #2 -> (Ok, 0xF00D, no-cap) — proves 0xCAFE was delivered before 0xF00D (per-direction FIFO).
7. EMPTY: RECV #3 on empty inbox -> WouldBlock.
8. BACKPRESSURE: fill C's outbox to bound (2 sends), the next send -> WouldBlock; assert no cap stranded (atomic check-before-detach).
9. NON-CHANNEL / DENIED paths: M_CHAN_SEND on agent_c's memory_home handle -> passes the WRITE gate but body sees ObjKind::MemoryHome -> BadCap (the `_ => err(BadCap)` arm, like M_MEM_READ on a non-memory object); M_CHAN_SEND on an endpoint NARROWed to remove WRITE -> Denied; a carried cap lacking TRANSFER -> Denied; unknown method 27 -> BadMethod.
10. Print intermediate witnesses ("ipc: FIFO + capability moved + attenuated", "ipc: full channel WouldBlock + non-channel BadCap") then the marker.

DOCS: docs/ROADMAP-V2.md §3 M14 (:228-247) + :381 status line; docs/AGENTS-SPEC.md §6 (capability-passing channel + ordered-replay stream).
risks: SINGLE-SCALAR SysReturn vs two outputs: recv must return BOTH the inline payload AND the moved capability handle, but SysReturn{status,value} holds one u64. Resolution: value = moved handle; payload via copy_to_user (real) or the kernel-side facade tuple agent_chan_recv->(status,payload,handle) (self-test). Don't try to pack both into one scalar.

ATOMICITY of the cap move on a full ring: if you detach the carried capability from the sender's table THEN find the outbox Ring full, the cap is stranded (lost, since it was free_slot'd). MUST check Ring capacity (would-not-block) BEFORE detach, or re-attach on WouldBlock. transfer_to is all-or-nothing; the split send/recv must preserve that.

BORROW aliasing: dispatch holds &mut table; reaching the endpoint's Rc<Channel> then borrow_mut()-ing its Ring while also detach/attach-ing on the same table can double-borrow. Must clone the endpoint Rc<Object> out and DROP the slot borrow before touching the Ring (the exact M13 M_MEM_* pattern), and finish the Ring borrow before table.attach. RefCell will panic at runtime, not compile-fail, if violated.

ZERO-new-unsafe scope boundary: the recon's inline-scalar increment is genuinely zero new unsafe (VecDeque+RefCell+Rc, all under forbid(unsafe_code)), but ROADMAP-V2.md:240 scopes M14 to include real BYTE payloads via copy_to_user/copy_from_user — the ONLY M14 unsafe. Be explicit that the marker-passing inline self-test deliberately defers the byte/user-buffer path; don't claim full ROADMAP M14 with zero unsafe.

Scheduler block/wake not testable with the timer disarmed: ROADMAP-V2.md:232 wants recv-on-empty to park off the M9 run queue and send to wake. The disarmed-timer kernel self-test can only observe the CLOSED-STATUS WouldBlock form, not the off-runqueue block + wake round-trip. State this so M14 isn't claimed to prove the IPC<->scheduler wake path it cannot exercise here.

TASK-SLOT budget: MAX_TASKS=12 (lib.rs:216) and slots 0..10 are consumed by boot/M2/M9/M10/M12/M13; only slot 11 is free. Two FRESH IPC agents overflow (agent_spawn fatals 'too many tasks'). Either REUSE agent_c/agent_d (slots 9,10 — already born with Channel bootstraps) or bump MAX_TASKS to >=14 and add STACK_AGENT_E/F.

Object field addition ripples: Object literal is built in 3 caps.rs constructors (mint, mint_memory_home, mint_record); adding `chan` requires updating all three with `chan: None` or the crate won't compile. Low risk but easy to miss the private mint_record.

Endpoint side bookkeeping: dir[side] is outbox, dir[1-side] is inbox. Getting the side flip wrong makes an agent read its own sends (echo) or deadlock. The two endpoints minted by connect MUST get side=0 and side=1 against the SAME Rc<Channel>.
